close_reason: Added UTF-8 path validation in from_uri, replaced to_string_lossy with expect
description: lib.rs:156 calls file_name.to_string_lossy() inside format!(). On filesystems with non-UTF-8 file names, U+FFFD substitution silently produces a tempfile name that does not round-trip. create_new(true) protects against the resulting collision, but the silent corruption of tmp_name is non-idiomatic for a security-sensitive library. Idiomatic alternative: format the tmp filename in OsString space using OsString::push, or fail explicitly on non-UTF-8 paths at from_uri. Filed as opinion because PathBuf-from-URI is already required to be UTF-8 by SecretUri::parse percent-decoding (secretx-core/src/lib.rs:1054), so this can only trigger if a non-UTF-8 path component arrived through some other means — not currently reachable.
title: tmp_name format string uses to_string_lossy — could surface UTF-8 errors as a path validation error instead
